summary: Short, permissive: do what you want, just keep the copyright notice. No patent grant.
Permissive with an explicit patent grant and termination-on-suit clause. Common for major OSS.
Permissive; like MIT plus a no-endorsement clause. No patent grant.
Permissive, simpler than BSD-3. No patent grant.
Permissive, functionally equivalent to MIT/BSD-2.
Strong copyleft: derivative works must be GPL-3.0. Patent grant + anti-tivoization.
Strong copyleft, older version. No explicit patent grant.
Strongest copyleft: derivative works AND network-service offerings must be AGPL.
Weak copyleft: linking is permitted; modifications to the LGPL library itself must be LGPL.
File-level copyleft: modifications to MPL-licensed files must be MPL, but the broader work may be other licenses.
Eclipse Public License — file-level copyleft, similar to MPL.
Public domain dedication.
Creative Commons CC0: waive all copyright. Public-domain-equivalent.
Creative Commons Attribution. Permissive for content/data with attribution requirement.
Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike. Copyleft for content/data — derivatives must share alike.
Attribution-NonCommercial. NOT redistribution-compatible for commercial use.
Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike. NOT for commercial use.
Attribution-NoDerivatives. Cannot modify.
Open RAIL-M: open weights with use-based restrictions (no military, no surveillance, etc).
BLOOM-style responsible-AI license with explicit use-case restrictions.
Meta Llama 2/3 community license. Free for most use, restrictions for very large operators.
MongoDB Server Side Public License — strong network copyleft; NOT OSI-approved.
Business Source License — source-available, becomes OSS after a delay (e.g. 4 years).
Elastic License — source-available, restrictions on competing managed services.
All rights reserved. Default if no license is declared.
